entry.notes: Single-event JSONL containing only a pre-compaction memory flush prompt — no tool_use events, no real user prompts, 15 seconds wall clock. Registry BUILD classification is wrong; this is a system-generated artefact, not a work session. The embedded conversation reveals the prior session was brain-dynamous setup work: Google OAuth for Gmail/Calendar, heartbeat.py testing, sqlite_vec fix, and architecture discussion around HEARTBEAT.md vs cron vs Supabase pg_cron. brain-dynamous is David's personal ambient AI OS (Python, uv, Claude Agent SDK, heartbeat-based reasoning, memory indexing). Key classifier finding: pre-compaction flush events must be detected early (look for 'Pre-compaction memory flush' in first user_prompt) and short-circuited to meta.compaction_flush before any tool-pattern analysis runs. Duration ≤30s is a secondary signal. Embedded conversation text has secondary attribution value if the prior session JSONL is missing.

entry.notes: Misleading event count — 6 registry events but 58KB of prior session transcript embedded in the single user_prompt event via resume-via-transcript-injection pattern. Real content: design and creation of the POEM YouTube Launch Optimizer (YLO) workflow YAML (442 lines) and 20 co-located JSON schemas for sections 1/2/4/5/6/7. BUILD classification is too coarse — no application code written; all artefacts are pipeline configuration. Correct subtype: build.config. Key signals: bash-heavy reflects git ops only, not compilation. Central design decision: transcriptAbridgement (1-4-abridge-v2) is the pipeline's most critical dependency (15+ consumers). Section 4 content analysis runs as action/llm-parallel producing 12-field shaped data. Two human checkpoint steps. Known open issue: Section 4 HBS templates return fenced code blocks not JSON — needs fixing before compiler run. Compiler not run in this session — explicitly deferred.
